item.title: Colgate must face lawsuits over safety of mouth rinse for young children
item.description: A federal judge on ​Friday said Colgate-Palmolive must face two lawsuits claiming its packaging for mouth ‌rinse misleads parents into believing children under 6 can use the products safely. The judge dismissed a similar lawsuit concerning Colgate toothpaste.

item.title: Dow confirms correction as traders worry about war
item.description: The Dow Jones Industrial Average fell 1.7% on Friday as investors worried about the ​global economic impact of the war in Iran, leaving the ‌blue-chip index down 10% from its record high close on February 10 and confirming it has been in a correction since then.

item.title: Refiners say new US biofuel quotas may worsen Iran war price spike
item.description: The Trump administration ordered U.S. refiners on Friday to increase the amount of biofuels they blend into gasoline and diesel this year and next, a move the industry said would only ​raise pump prices already spiking due to the war in Iran.

item.title: Expansion of Disneyland Paris will create 1,000 new jobs
item.description: The opening of a new site at Disneyland Paris - ​which has become one of ‌Europe's main tourist attractions since it launched in 1992 - will create 1,000 new ​jobs, said Disney CEO Josh D'Amaro ​and French President Emmanuel Macron on ⁠Friday.

item.title: Oil, gas exploration is back: Energy giants hunt to replenish reserves
item.description: Global energy companies are getting back to basics and focusing on the hunt for new sources of oil and gas, executives declared at the CERAWeek conference in Houston this week, ending years of underinvestment in exploration.

item.title: Convenience store owner Yesway moves closer to public markets with US IPO filing
item.description: Yesway revived its U.S. initial public offering on Friday, moving the private equity-backed convenience store owner ​closer to public markets.

item.title: Under Trump, Wall Street regulator's headcount falls 18%, watchdog says
item.description: Nearly one in five staff members had departed Wall Street's top regulator by ​September of last year under normal attrition and the Trump administration's campaign of job ‌cuts, with losses falling most heavily on divisions overseeing investment managers and stock markets, an independent congressional watchdog reported Friday.

item.title: California bans officials from using inside knowledge to bet on prediction markets
item.description: California Governor Gavin Newsom on Friday issued an executive ​order barring state officials from using ‌insider knowledge to bet on prediction markets like Polymarket and Kalshi.

item.title: Apple hires ex-Google executive to head AI marketing amid push to improve Siri
item.description: Apple on Friday ​said it has ‌hired Lilian Rincon, who previously spent ​nearly a decade ​at Google overseeing its ⁠shopping and ​assistant products, as the ​vice president of product marketing for artificial ​intelligence, reporting to ​its marketing chief Greg “Joz” ‌Joswiak.

item.title: Services firms feel the squeeze as oil rally from Iran war fails to spur drilling
item.description: Global oilfield services companies are bracing for a hit to earnings as the Iran war disrupts energy infrastructure across the Middle East and producers hold back ​on new drilling until higher oil prices prove durable.

item.title: Trump tells farmers that tractor companies should lower prices
item.description: President Donald Trump announced new measures on Friday to support U.S. farmers who are reeling from the ‌administration's trade policies and the Iran war and suggested farm equipment makers cut prices - a call that sent their shares lower.

item.title: EU Commission web platform hit by cyber-attack on March 24
item.description: The European Commission was targeted ​by a cyber attack ‌on March 24 affecting its cloud infrastructure ​hosting the Europa ​web platform, with early findings ⁠suggesting data have ​been taken from those ​websites, the Commission said on Friday.

item.title: Citigroup said to weigh buying US regional bank, Bloomberg News reports
item.description: Citigroup's top executives are ​weighing buying a U.S. regional ‌bank, Bloomberg News reported on Friday, as the Wall Street ​giant looks to wrap ​up a years-long effort to streamline ⁠its sprawling operations.

item.title: Exclusive: US sends subpoenas in Warner-Paramount antitrust review as probe picks up steam
item.description: The U.S. Department of Justice has sent subpoenas in its investigation of Paramount Skydance's acquisition of Warner Bros ​Discovery , three sources familiar with the matter told Reuters.

item.title: Fed's Paulson says Iran war increases risks to growth, inflation
item.description: Philadelphia Federal Reserve President Anna Paulson said on Friday the Iran war is creating challenges for ​the U.S. economy, while stopping short of saying what ‌that might mean for near-term changes in monetary policy.

item.title: Fed's Barkin says 'fog' again obscuring economic outlook
item.description: The U.S. war with Iran and the rapid roll-out of artificial intelligence technologies have again clouded the outlook for the Federal Reserve, and make ​it appropriate to keep interest rates on hold for now, Richmond Fed ‌President Thomas Barkin said on Friday.

item.title: Unilever shareholders to get majority stake in potential McCormick food deal, sources say
item.description: A proposed combination of Unilever's food business with U.S. spice maker McCormick ​would offer the British group's shareholders a majority stake in the new entity and tax benefits, two people familiar with the ‌matter told Reuters.

item.title: Europe's aviation agency touts improving ties with FAA, Boeing
item.description: Europe's top aviation regulator signalled improved relations with the U.S. FAA and said Boeing was responding "in ​the appropriate manner" to regulatory scrutiny, suggesting recent transatlantic tensions ‌over aviation safety have eased.

item.title: Chile's Codelco logs $4.85 billion pre-tax profit in 'very difficult' 2025
item.description: Chilean state-owned Codelco [RIC:RIC:COBRE.UL], the world's largest copper producer, on Friday reported a pre-tax profit of $4.85 billion in 2025.

item.title: Utility Entergy says revised Meta data-center deal to deliver higher customer savings
item.description: Utility Entergy said Meta Platforms will pay its full cost of service for ​the planned hyperscale data center in ‌northeast Louisiana under a revised agreement.

item.title: WTO at 'critical juncture' and needs deep reform, EU and CPTPP say
item.description: The World Trade Organisation is at a "critical juncture" and needs deep ​reform, the European Union and parties to the Comprehensive and ‌Progressive Agreement for Trans-Pacific Partnership said on Friday.

item.title: US consumer sentiment hits three-month low as war stokes inflation fears
item.description: U.S. consumer sentiment fell more than expected in March touching a three-month low, as war in the Middle East stoked inflation ​worries and cast a shadow over the economic outlook.

item.title: Iran-linked hackers breach FBI director's personal email, publish excerpts online
item.description: Iran-linked hackers on Friday claimed they had accessed ​FBI Director Kash Patel's personal email inbox, publishing photographs of the ‌director and other documents to the internet.

item.title: US appeals court voids $16.1 billion judgment against Argentina over YPF seizure
item.description: A divided U.S. appeals ‌court on Friday struck down a $16.1 billion judgment against Argentina for seizing control of state-owned oil company YPF in 2012, a victory for Argentine President Javier Milei as he tries to stabilize the country's long-strained economy.

item.title: Sony to hike PlayStation 5 prices again as memory chip costs surge
item.description: Sony Group is raising global prices of its PlayStation 5 consoles, including a $100 ​increase in the U.S., marking its second hike ‌in less than a year as the Japanese firm grapples with rising costs of key components such as memory chips.

item.title: Cruise operator Carnival cuts annual profit forecast as fuel costs surge
item.description: Carnival Corp cut its annual profit forecast on Friday, ​as higher fuel costs pressure the cruise operator's margins amid rising geopolitical ‌tensions.

item.title: Markets in Q1: Everything everywhere all at once
item.description: The first quarter of the year comes to an end next week after an extraordinary few months where the world's financial markets have been tossed around at the mercy of geopolitics, ​with the Iran war wiping $7 trillion off global stocks.

item.title: Middle East conflict drives record trading at NYSE-parent ICE
item.description: Shockwaves from the Middle East conflict have rippled through global markets, driving record trading at Intercontinental Exchange across energy, commodities, futures and options.

item.title: Emerging economies' record debt spree slumps into a freeze as Iran war rocks markets
item.description: A record start to the year for emerging-market debt sales has largely ground to a halt as worries ​over the Iran war create havoc in the markets and push up borrowing costs, bankers and investors told Reuters, a situation that places a ‌number of nations in limbo.

item.title: NYSE-parent Intercontinental Exchange invests $600 million in Polymarket
item.description: Intercontinental Exchange said on Friday it had invested $600 ​million in prediction markets platform Polymarket, ‌as the New York Stock Exchange parent expands into the fast-growing event-based trading segment.
